questions.question: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra was chosen as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne in 1908.
questions.trick: It's an inland constructed city perfectly between the two coast majors.

questions.question:
Statements:
1. All Dogs are Cats.
2. All Cats are Animals.

Which conclusion logically follows?
questions.explanation: If A is a subset of B, and B is a subset of C, then A must be a subset of C completely.
questions.trick: Draw a venn diagram: Small circle A inside Medium B inside Large C.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: At about 6,650 km long, it flows into the Mediterranean Sea.
questions.trick: Amazon is largest by volume, Nile is longest by length.

questions.question: In which year did Mahatma Gandhi return to India from South Africa?
questions.explanation: Gandhi returned to India on Jan 9, 1915 (Pravasi Bharatiya Divas).
questions.trick: Remember 1915 as the start of his Indian movements.

questions.question: What will be the Simple Interest on a principal amount of Rs. 16400 at an interest rate of 11% per annum over 3 years?
Who has been appointed as the new Chief of the Naval Staff of India, taking charge from April 30, 2024?
questions.explanation: Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi took over as the 26th Chief of the Naval Staff on April 30, 2024, succeeding Admiral R. Hari Kumar.
questions.trick: Other options are former chiefs of the Naval Staff or incorrect names.

questions.question: What is the average of the following 5 consecutive even numbers: 46, 48, 50, 52, 54?
questions.trick: For a consecutive arithmetic sequence, the average is simply the exact middle term.

questions.question: Which new payment facility did the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) recently enable for non-bank Payment System Operators (PSOs) and their customers, effective from March 15, 2024?
options: UPI access through Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPIs)
questions.correct_answer: UPI access through Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPIs)
questions.explanation: The RBI permitted non-bank PSOs to offer UPI using their existing Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPIs) such as wallets, enhancing convenience for users.
questions.trick: Other options represent different aspects of digital payments, some of which might be under discussion or already exist in different forms.

questions.question: Who among the following were recently appointed as the new Election Commissioners of India in March 2024?
options: Gyanesh Kumar and Sukhbir Singh Sandhu
Sukhbir Singh Sandhu and T.V. Somanathan
Rajiv Kumar and Anup Chandra Pandey
questions.correct_answer: Gyanesh Kumar and Sukhbir Singh Sandhu
questions.explanation: Gyanesh Kumar and Sukhbir Singh Sandhu were appointed as Election Commissioners on March 14, 2024, to fill vacancies in the Election Commission.
questions.trick: Rajiv Kumar is the Chief Election Commissioner. Anup Chandra Pandey retired. Other names are high-ranking officials but not Election Commissioners.

questions.question: If BAT is coded as EDW, how will PEN be coded in that same language?
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by exactly 3 positions in the English alphabet.
questions.trick: Note the position gap between the first letter of the word and the first letter of its code.

questions.question: Which university emerged as the overall champion at the 4th Khelo India University Games 2023, held in Guwahati, Assam, which concluded in February 2024?
questions.explanation: Chandigarh University secured the top position at the 4th Khelo India University Games 2023 (KIUG 2023), which concluded on February 29, 2024, with a total of 71 medals including 32 gold.
questions.trick: The other options are also strong contenders in such games or have won previously, making it a test of recent recall.

questions.question: What does 'SURAJ' stand for in the recently launched PM-SURAJ portal, a national portal for beneficiaries of schemes related to Scheduled Castes (SCs)?
questions.explanation: PM-SURAJ stands for 'Pradhan Mantri - Samagra Anusuchit Jati Utkarsh Sahbhagita'. The portal and scheme were launched by the Prime Minister in March 2024.
questions.trick: The options are designed to sound plausible, testing recall of the exact acronym.

questions.question: If an article is bought for Rs. 1960 and sold at a profit of 15%, what is the selling price?
questions.trick: Calculate 10% of CP, multiply, and add it conceptually.

questions.question: A can complete a piece of work in 48 days while B can complete the same work in 108 days. If they work together, in how many days will they complete the work?
questions.explanation: Work = 48 * 108 = 5184. Rate = 48 + 108 = 156. Time = Work / Rate = 33.23 days.

questions.question: Who among the following eminent personalities was posthumously conferred the Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honour, during the recent Padma Awards ceremony in April 2024?
questions.explanation: Dr. Bindeshwar Pathak, founder of Sulabh International, was posthumously awarded the Padma Vibhushan. All other options also received Padma Vibhushan in 2024 but were not posthumous.
questions.trick: All options are Padma Vibhushan recipients for 2024, but only one posthumously. This tests specific recall.

questions.question: Find the next number in the series: 14, 29, 44, 59, 74, ?
questions.explanation: The series follows an Arithmetic Progression with a constant difference of 15. So, 74 + (15) = 89.
questions.trick: Always check the difference between the first and second terms and verify across the series.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for Gold?
questions.explanation: Au comes from the Latin word 'Aurum'.

questions.question: Find the odd one out among the following numbers: 101, 25, 49, 36
questions.explanation: 25, 36, and 49 are perfect squares. 101 is not a perfect square.
questions.trick: Memorize squares up to 30 to instantly spot discrepancies.
